differential_diagnoses: Head and neck malignancy (e.g., squamous cell carcinoma or high-grade sarcoma)
next_steps: Contrast-enhanced CT angiography (CTA) or MRA to rule out pseudoaneurysm of the internal carotid or ascending pharyngeal artery
Direct laryngoscopy and biopsy of the lesion under anesthesia
Pathological examination of biopsy tissue including immunohistochemistry and cultures
Systemic imaging (PET/CT) for staging if malignancy is identified
Consultation with vascular surgery and head and neck oncology
